translations.code.agentFirewall.activityDesc: A live operations list for agent tool calls, protected servers, and control work that must close before enforcement can be trusted.

translations.code.agentFirewall.attackLabDesc: Replay controlled agent attack chains through the same ingest and policy path used by production agents. The first probe in a chain should be sent with executed=true so the behavior memory closes the loop before the second probe is judged.
translations.code.agentFirewall.attackLabKeyNote: Use an API key with the mcp:invoke scope. The product name is Agent Firewall; the current transport endpoint is still MCP-compatible for backward compatibility.
translations.code.agentFirewall.label: Agent Firewall
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerAccessManager: Block high-risk AI services, require exception, or allow approved private endpoints.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerCodeJoin: attribute coding-agent activity to repos and projects
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerDeploymentManag: Support cloud, on-prem connected, restricted egress, and full air-gapped rollout paths.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerDesc: A buyer-ready control plane for Shadow AI, AI DLP, Agent Firewall runtime enforcement, evidence export, and enterprise deployment. It turns the meeting checklist into one operational loop.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerDlpCasbJoin: correlate AI prompt/file leakage with existing controls
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerDlpManager: Stop raw PII, secrets, customer data, code, files, or screenshots from reaching external AI.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerEvidenceManager: Show who did what, which policy fired, what was transformed, and what remains open for audit.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerHeadline: Govern AI usage before data leaves, agents mutate systems, or audit asks for proof.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerIdentityJoin: join user identity to AI activity
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerLede: This is not only AI governance and not only security tooling. It is a runtime security layer plus management evidence: discover usage, classify risk, decide policy, transform sensitive content, enforce, and report.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerModelJoin: allow approved model endpoints and restrict unknown external AI
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerOfflineJoin: ship signed rules, CVE/threat intel, connector updates, and license files
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerPocStep1Desc: Deploy to a pilot group and collect Shadow AI, browser, and agent runtime telemetry without blocking.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerPocStep2Desc: Map AI apps, models, tools, data classes, permission scopes, and target trust into policy.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerPocStep3Desc: Replay events against policy to show what would be blocked, held, tokenized, or approved.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerPocStep4Desc: Move approved controls into soft or hard enforcement and keep audit evidence exportable.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerRuntimeManager: Control Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, MCP, internal LLM apps, and tool-calling agents at execution time.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerShadowManager: Know which sanctioned, unsanctioned, and unknown AI apps are used by user and device.
translations.code.agentFirewall.centerSiemJoin: send digest-safe violations and policy actions

translations.code.agentFirewall.controlTrafficOkDesc: Recent non-diagnostic decisions are landing in the decision log.

translations.code.agentFirewall.dimToolDesc: Read, write, scan, browser automation, shell execution.

translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.data_protec: Sensitive data, outbound movement, blocking, and tokenization readiness are visible without storing raw payloads.
translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.identity_ap: Runtime evidence identifies user, device, app, and agent context for audit.
translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.inference_c: Detects when harmless-looking fragments can combine into sensitive conclusions across a session or project.
translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.inventory_c: Known AI use cases, models, frameworks, and runtime surfaces are registered.
translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.ownership_a: Each AI use case has business and technical accountability.
translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.policy_enfo: Org rollout mode and use-case policies show how governance becomes runtime control.
translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.risk_classi: Use cases carry risk tiers, data classes, and stricter handling for high-risk AI.
translations.code.agentFirewall.dimension.runtime_tel: Agent and AI-app actions are visible in the same decision and evidence path.

translations.code.agentFirewall.dlpDesc: Prevent sensitive content from reaching external AI systems while preserving useful context through stable tokenization.

translations.code.agentFirewall.enterprise.backend_co: Backend owns the full control loop: lifecycle, policy, ingest, replay, DLP evidence, timeline, and inference-risk scoring.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterprise.database_e: Persistent evidence is append-only, digest-safe, indexed, and replayable for audit without raw payload storage.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterprise.enterprise: Cloud, on-prem connected, restricted-egress, and air-gapped profiles let MIS point connectors at the right Warroom and engine location.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterprise.identity_d: SSO/SAML, SCIM, RBAC, API-key scopes, owners, users, devices, and apps join into the runtime evidence trail.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterprise.runtime_co: Installable connectors cover MCP proxy, endpoint relay, browser telemetry, heartbeat, and Windows/macOS/Linux deployment paths.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterprise.ui_coverag: Management and engineering surfaces cover discovery, governance, DLP, attack replay, activity review, and evidence export.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterprise.validation: Attack Lab replay, decision replay, retention policy/status, legal hold, store tests, and route-drift checks close the operations loop.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterpriseEmpty: No enterprise readiness score is available yet.
translations.code.agentFirewall.enterpriseReadinessDe: Computed from UI coverage, backend control depth, database evidence durability, runtime connectors, deployment packaging, identity integration, and operations validation. Each score must be backed by runtime signals and audit-safe evidence.
translations.code.agentFirewall.evidenceDesc: Operator-facing reports for who used AI, what policy fired, what was blocked or transformed, and what evidence can be shown safely.
translations.code.agentFirewall.exportLoadsFromBacken: Export loads from the backend evidence report API.

translations.code.agentFirewall.governanceEvidenceDes: Digest-safe reports prove who did what, what policy fired, and what remediation remains.
translations.code.agentFirewall.governanceInventoryDe: Shadow AI and use case register identify which teams, apps, and models are in scope.
translations.code.agentFirewall.governanceReadinessDe: Score is calculated from weighted governance dimensions, each backed by signals and evidence.
translations.code.agentFirewall.intro: Agent Firewall governs agent tool calls: a digest-only decision log (no raw payloads/secrets) plus egress and side-effect policy. Configuring the policy requires the mcp:configure capability.

translations.code.agentFirewall.scopeNote: What this protects: agent tool calls routed through the firewall proxy. It can block sensitive-data egress and dangerous side-effects — but only in an enforcing mode, and only for traffic that actually passes through the proxy. MCP is one supported transport, not the product boundary.
translations.code.agentFirewall.shadowDesc: Discover and govern sanctioned and unsanctioned AI applications by user, device, app, and policy outcome.
translations.code.agentFirewall.shadowEndpointGap: Endpoint/browser telemetry is the next connector needed for full user-side Shadow AI discovery. Runtime Agent Firewall telemetry is already wired for routed agents.

translations.code.agentFirewall.stepKey: Create an Agent Firewall ingest key

translations.code.agentFirewall.stepVerifyDesc: Use the dashboard probe below for a safe synthetic event, then send the curl probe or a real agent tool call. Both paths land in Recent Agent Firewall decisions.

translations.code.agentFirewall.tokenizationDesc: Stable typed tokens keep repeated entities consistent for LLM reasoning without exposing raw email, phone, ID, or secret values.
translations.code.agentFirewall.tokenizationWorkbench: Same entity, same org, same typed token. The LLM can still reason about repeated entities without seeing the raw value.
translations.code.agentFirewall.userDeviceAttribution: The ingest contract now accepts userId, deviceId, appName, appCategory, actionType, and permissionScope so endpoint and browser agents can attribute Shadow AI usage.

translations.code.dast.check.notMailDomain: Not a mail domain (no MX) — SPF / DMARC / DKIM not applicable
translations.code.dast.clickjackingDesc: Without X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors, attackers can embed your site in an iframe for clickjacking attacks.
translations.code.dast.cookieNoEncryptDesc: A cookie is set without the 'secure' flag, meaning it can be intercepted over HTTP connections.
translations.code.dast.cookieNoHttpDesc: A cookie without HttpOnly can be accessed by JavaScript, enabling session theft via XSS.
translations.code.dast.cookiePoisonDesc: URL parameters can set cookie values, which may be exploitable for session manipulation.
translations.code.dast.cookieSubdomainDesc: Authentication cookies scoped to parent domain can be accessed by any subdomain, increasing attack surface.
translations.code.dast.cspEvalDesc: CSP does not block eval(), setTimeout() and other functions that can execute unknown code, enabling code injection.
translations.code.dast.cspFallbackDesc: CSP does not define a fallback for a directive, causing the browser to treat that content type as unrestricted.
translations.code.dast.cspInlineCssDesc: CSP allows inline CSS, which can aid social engineering attacks by injecting misleading visual elements.
translations.code.dast.cspInlineJsDesc: CSP is configured but allows inline JavaScript, which is a common XSS attack vector.
translations.code.dast.cspNotSet: Content Security Policy (CSP) header not set
translations.code.dast.cspNotSetDesc: CSP is the first line of defense against XSS and data injection attacks. It controls which content sources are allowed to load and execute.
translations.code.dast.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? All discovery data, subdomains, and scan results will be permanently removed.
translations.code.dast.dirBrowsingDesc: Directory listings are enabled, potentially revealing hidden scripts, backup files, and sensitive information.
translations.code.dast.emptyDesc: Add your domains to discover exposed services, SSL status, DNS records, tech stacks, and potential vulnerabilities across your external perimeter.
translations.code.dast.envExposedDesc: Environment files expose infrastructure credentials, API keys, or other sensitive configuration information.
translations.code.dast.graphqlIntrospectDesc: GraphQL introspection reveals the entire API schema including all types, queries, and mutations.
translations.code.dast.graphqlWarning: Introspection is enabled — this exposes your full schema to anyone. Disable in production.
translations.code.dast.heartbleedDesc: OpenSSL Heartbeat Extension vulnerability allows remote attackers to read process memory, potentially disclosing sensitive information.
translations.code.dast.hstsNotSet: TLS not enforced with valid HSTS header
translations.code.dast.hstsNotSetDesc: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) forces browsers to use HTTPS only. Without it, users are vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks via protocol downgrade.
translations.code.dast.httpOnlyDesc: The site responds on HTTP without redirecting to HTTPS, exposing traffic to interception and modification.
translations.code.dast.jwtInvalidDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens with invalid signatures, allowing attackers to modify token payload without detection.
translations.code.dast.jwtNoneDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens signed with the 'none' algorithm, bypassing signature verification entirely.
translations.code.dast.jwtSelfSignedDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens with self-signed JWK keys, allowing attackers to impersonate legitimate users.
translations.code.dast.jwtWeakDesc: The JWT token returned by your server uses a weak or known compromised secret, allowing attackers to forge tokens for unauthorized access.
translations.code.dast.linkRepoHint: Link a repository to filter API routes
translations.code.dast.noApiFindings: No API documentation, GraphQL, or JS bundle endpoints detected.
translations.code.dast.noCaaDesc: No CAA record restricts which certificate authorities may issue certificates for this domain.
translations.code.dast.noDmarcDesc: Without DMARC, there is no policy to handle emails that fail SPF/DKIM checks, enabling phishing attacks using your domain.
translations.code.dast.noDnssecDesc: Without DNSSEC, DNS responses can be spoofed, potentially redirecting users to malicious servers.
translations.code.dast.noHstsPreloadDesc: The domain is not on the HSTS preload list, so the first visit can be downgraded to HTTP.
translations.code.dast.noPageSpeed: No PageSpeed data yet. Run discovery to test.
translations.code.dast.noSniffDesc: Without 'nosniff', browsers may interpret files as a different MIME type, enabling XSS via MIME-type confusion.
translations.code.dast.noSpfDesc: Without SPF (Sender Policy Framework), attackers can send emails appearing to come from your domain (email spoofing).
translations.code.dast.notVerified: Not verified yet — run discovery to test
translations.code.dast.openPortDesc: A service port is publicly accessible that should typically be behind a firewall. Database, cache, and remote access ports exposed to the internet are high-risk attack vectors.
translations.code.dast.poweredByLeakDesc: The server exposes the framework/runtime it uses, helping attackers identify specific vulnerabilities
translations.code.dast.psHintA11y: Missing alt text, contrast issues, or ARIA labels.
translations.code.dast.psHintBP: Using deprecated APIs or insecure resources.
translations.code.dast.psHintPerf: Slow loading. Optimize images, lazy-load, reduce JS bundle.
translations.code.dast.psHintSEO: Missing meta tags, no sitemap, or poor mobile usability.
translations.code.dast.sensitiveFilesCriticalDesc: Critical sensitive files are publicly reachable
translations.code.dast.sensitiveFilesDesc: These files are publicly accessible and may leak source code, configuration, or credentials.
translations.code.dast.serverLeak: Server leaks info via 'Server' HTTP header
translations.code.dast.serverLeakDesc: The server is exposing its software identity via the Server header. This helps attackers identify known vulnerabilities
translations.code.dast.sessionCookieDesc: The session cookie is missing 'httpOnly' and 'secure' attributes, making it vulnerable to theft by third-party scripts.
translations.code.dast.sessionStorage: Session token found in browser storage
translations.code.dast.sessionStorageDesc: Session tokens stored in localStorage/sessionStorage have no protection against injected third-party scripts.
translations.code.dast.sourceCodeGitDesc: Git repository files are publicly accessible, potentially exposing source code and sensitive configuration.
translations.code.dast.sriMissingDesc: External scripts/styles loaded without integrity attributes can be tampered with if the CDN is compromised.
translations.code.dast.sslExpiryDesc: The TLS certificate is about to expire, which will break HTTPS connections and trigger browser warnings.

translations.code.legal.beta.feature.aiSandbox: AI Sandbox (flyto-ai) — AI-generated code execution and self-repair workflows
translations.code.legal.beta.feature.autofix: AutoFix Tier 2 — AI-proposed code patches (always generates a PR for review; never auto-merges)
translations.code.legal.beta.feature.cloudCspm: Cloud CSPM — Cloud security posture management (limited availability)
translations.code.legal.beta.feature.mcpGuardian: MCP Guardian — Model Context Protocol agent firewall and policy engine
translations.code.legal.beta.feedback.prefix: Beta users can report issues and provide feedback at
translations.code.legal.beta.feedback.suffix: . Bug reports from beta users directly influence release prioritization.

translations.code.legal.beta.meaning.breakingChanges: Change significantly between releases without backward compatibility
translations.code.legal.beta.meaning.falsePositives: Produce false positives or miss real vulnerabilities — all scanner output should be reviewed by a qualified security professional before action is taken
translations.code.legal.beta.meaning.intro: Features marked with a "Beta" badge in the UI are preview releases. They may:
translations.code.legal.beta.meaning.limits: Be subject to rate limits, downtime, or data loss during major upgrades
translations.code.legal.beta.meaning.title: What "Beta" means
translations.code.legal.beta.sla.body: Beta features are explicitly excluded from any SLA commitments. We target 99.5% uptime for stable features and make commercially reasonable efforts for beta features. Planned maintenance windows will be announced at least 24 hours in advance via email.
translations.code.legal.beta.sla.title: SLA during Beta
translations.code.legal.beta.stable.attackSurface: External attack surface scanning and scoring
translations.code.legal.beta.stableFeatures.intro: The following features are considered stable and suitable for production use:
translations.code.legal.beta.stableFeatures.title: Stable Features
translations.code.legal.beta.title: Beta Program Disclaimer
translations.code.legal.beta.warning: Flyto2 is currently in public beta. Some features are still under active development and may not be suitable for production security workflows without human review.
translations.code.legal.privacy.accountInfo.body: When you create an account we collect your name, email address, and authentication credentials (managed by Firebase Auth). If you sign in via GitHub or Google OAuth, we receive the profile information those providers share with us.
translations.code.legal.privacy.accountInfo.label: Account information:
translations.code.legal.privacy.contactPrefix: Questions about this policy should be directed to
translations.code.legal.privacy.repoData.body: When you connect a repository, we clone a temporary copy to perform security analysis. We do not retain source code beyond the duration of the scan unless you explicitly request persistent indexing.
translations.code.legal.privacy.repoData.label: Repository and code data:
translations.code.legal.privacy.scanResults.body: We store the output of security scans (findings, scores, metadata) associated with your organization. This data is retained for 90 days by default and is never shared with third parties.
translations.code.legal.privacy.scanResults.label: Scan results:
translations.code.legal.privacy.storage.body: Customer data is stored on Google Cloud Platform in the asia-east1 (Taiwan) region. Data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256). We employ role-based access control and hash-chained audit logging for all privileged operations. Execution traces are redacted of secrets before persistence.
translations.code.legal.privacy.subprocessorsIntro: We use the following sub-processors to deliver the Service:
translations.code.legal.privacy.usageData.body: We collect standard server logs (IP address, user agent, request path, response code, timestamp) to operate and improve the Service. We do not use third-party analytics SDKs.
translations.code.legal.privacy.usageData.label: Usage data:
translations.code.legal.security.disclosure.credit: Credit researchers who report valid vulnerabilities (with permission)
translations.code.legal.security.disclosure.encryptPr: Please encrypt sensitive reports using our
translations.code.legal.security.disclosure.encryptSu: . Do not disclose vulnerabilities publicly until we have had a chance to address them.
translations.code.legal.security.disclosure.intro: If you discover a security vulnerability in any Flyto2 product, service, or infrastructure, please report it responsibly. We commit to:
translations.code.legal.security.disclosure.resolve: Resolve critical and high-severity issues within 30 days
translations.code.legal.security.disclosure.title: Vulnerability Disclosure
translations.code.legal.security.disclosure.update: Provide a status update within 7 days
translations.code.legal.security.incident.assess: Assess — determine scope and impact within 4 hours
translations.code.legal.security.incident.contain: Contain — isolate affected systems within 1 hour of detection
translations.code.legal.security.incident.intro: We maintain an internal incident response runbook. In the event of a breach or significant security incident affecting customer data, we will:
translations.code.legal.security.incident.notify: Notify — inform affected customers within 72 hours as required under GDPR Art. 33/34
translations.code.legal.security.incident.remediate: Remediate — patch the root cause and verify fix
translations.code.legal.security.incident.report: Report — publish a post-mortem for incidents affecting multiple customers
translations.code.legal.security.incident.title: Incident Response
translations.code.legal.security.practice.auditLoggin: Every privileged action writes a hash-chained audit entry. The chain integrity is verifiable via GET /audit?verify=true.
translations.code.legal.security.practice.authenticat: Firebase Auth handles identity. API access requires a valid Firebase ID token on every request.
translations.code.legal.security.practice.encryptionR: Database volumes and file storage are encrypted at rest using AES-256.
translations.code.legal.security.practice.encryptionT: All traffic is TLS 1.2+ enforced. HSTS is set on all production endpoints.
translations.code.legal.security.practice.rbac.body: Four roles (owner / admin / member / guest) with per-surface permission gates. Sensitive operations require admin.
translations.code.legal.security.practice.rbac.title: Role-Based Access Control
translations.code.legal.security.practice.secretRedac: Execution traces and evidence are redacted before persistence — API keys, tokens, cookies, JWTs, and PEM keys are masked.
translations.code.legal.security.practice.staticAnaly: CodeQL scans run on every pull request across all repos. Gitleaks performs secret scanning on full commit history.
translations.code.legal.security.practice.supplyChain: All dependencies are scanned with Grype on every release. Container images are scanned with Trivy. SLSA provenance is attached to every PyPI release.
translations.code.legal.security.practices.title: How We Secure the Platform
translations.code.legal.security.scope.intro: The following are in scope for responsible disclosure:
translations.code.legal.security.scope.outOfScope: Out of scope: third-party services we integrate with, social engineering attacks, denial-of-service, and attacks requiring physical access.
translations.code.legal.security.scope.title: Scope
translations.code.legal.security.subtitle: We build security tooling. That means our own security practices need to meet the same bar we hold our customers to.
translations.code.legal.security.title: Security at Flyto2
translations.code.legal.terms.permittedUseIntro: You may use the Service solely for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree not to:
translations.code.legal.trust.contact.title: Security questions or concerns?
translations.code.legal.trust.control.codeql: CodeQL static analysis on all pull requests
translations.code.legal.trust.control.firebaseAuth: Firebase Auth with ID-token verification on every request
translations.code.legal.trust.control.gitleaks: Gitleaks secret scanning on full commit history
translations.code.legal.trust.control.grype: Grype CVE scanning on every release (SBOM-backed)
translations.code.legal.trust.control.rbac: Role-based access control (owner / admin / member / guest)
translations.code.legal.trust.control.secretRedaction: Secret redaction on all evidence before persistence
translations.code.legal.trust.control.shaPinning: Dependency SHA pinning in all CI workflows
translations.code.legal.trust.control.slsa: SLSA provenance attestation on all PyPI releases
translations.code.legal.trust.control.trivy: Trivy container image scanning (HIGH/CRITICAL, SARIF to GitHub)
translations.code.legal.trust.dataPractices.credentia: We never store plaintext credentials. API keys are hashed (SHA-256) before persistence. Execution traces are redacted of tokens, cookies, and PEM keys before storage.
translations.code.legal.trust.dataPractices.isolation: Each organization's data is isolated at the database level using org_id scoping. Row-level security is enforced on every query.
Customer data is stored on Google Cloud Platform in the asia-east1 (Taiwan) region by default. Data does not leave this region without explicit customer consent.
translations.code.legal.trust.dataPractices.retention: Execution traces and scan evidence are retained for 90 days by default. Customers can configure shorter retention or request immediate deletion.
translations.code.legal.trust.dataPractices.secretSca: Flyto-indexer scans for accidentally committed secrets. The engine's redaction layer prevents any secret detected in execution output from reaching disk.
translations.code.legal.trust.dataPractices.thirdPart: We do not sell customer data. We share data with sub-processors only to the extent necessary to provide the service (Cloud Run, Cloud SQL, Firebase Auth).
translations.code.legal.trust.dataPractices.title: Data Practices
translations.code.legal.trust.platformStatus.desc: Current status of all Flyto2 services.
translations.code.legal.trust.platformStatus.title: Platform Status
translations.code.legal.trust.policyDocuments.title: Policy Documents
translations.code.legal.trust.securityControls.title: Security Controls

translations.code.mcp.egress.emptyHint: Outbound calls carrying secrets, credentials, PII or source code will appear here.

translations.code.mcp.egressCleanDesc: No sensitive-data egress has been seen across your MCP tool calls.
translations.code.mcp.explain.drift: The current policy would decide differently than what was recorded at ingest.
translations.code.mcp.explain.title: Why was this flagged?
translations.code.mcp.intro: MCP Guardian governs agent tool-calls: a digest-only decision log (no raw payloads/secrets) plus the egress/tool-call policy. Configuring the policy requires the mcp:configure capability.
translations.code.mcp.mode.enforce.desc: Block everything the floor rules deny.
translations.code.mcp.mode.enforce.label: Enforce
translations.code.mcp.mode.observe.desc: Log only — never compute a blocking verdict.
translations.code.mcp.mode.observe.label: Observe
translations.code.mcp.mode.shadow.desc: Compute verdicts, record them, but never block.
translations.code.mcp.mode.shadow.label: Shadow
translations.code.mcp.mode.soft_enforce.desc: Block writes / egress only; reads always proceed.
translations.code.mcp.mode.soft_enforce.label: Soft enforce
translations.code.mcp.modeEnforceSub: Actively blocking policy violations in real time.
translations.code.mcp.modeObserveSub: Watching and logging decisions only — NOT blocking anything yet.
translations.code.mcp.modeShadowSub: Evaluating policy in shadow — recording what it WOULD block, but not blocking yet.
translations.code.mcp.modeSoftSub: Blocks the highest-risk calls; warns on the rest.
translations.code.mcp.noRegistryButEvents: The guardian has recorded MCP events, but no static server inventory has been synced yet.
translations.code.mcp.noRegistryDiagnosticOnly: Only the dashboard diagnostic probe has been recorded so far — no live agent traffic or server inventory yet. Point a real MCP proxy at the ingest endpoint to start streaming events.
translations.code.mcp.noRegistryYet: No MCP server inventory is registered yet. Send the dashboard probe or direct ingest probe above to verify the data plane.
translations.code.mcp.notConfiguredHint: Connect the guardian agent to start recording tool-call decisions.
translations.code.mcp.notWiredNote: No MCP server is registered yet. Once the guardian sees traffic, servers, tools, and decisions show up here.
translations.code.mcp.placeholderDesc: Runtime guardrails for MCP servers and agent tool calls — policy enforcement, attempt logging, and anomaly detection across your agent fleet.
translations.code.mcp.placeholderNote: The guardian engine is being wired to the live gateway. This surface lights up automatically once it ships.
translations.code.mcp.policyJsonHelp: A serialized guardian policy. Leave blank for mode-only config.
translations.code.mcp.policyLede: Move the guardian along the safety ramp. The seven floor safety rules always evaluate; the rollout mode controls whether a DENY actually blocks the agent.
translations.code.mcp.proxyEnv: For flyto-security-mcp, set FLYTO_ENGINE_URL to this engine host and enable FLYTO_MCP_USE_INGEST=1 so decisions come from this backend policy.
translations.code.mcp.scopeNote: What this protects: MCP tool calls routed through the guardian proxy. It can block sensitive-data egress and dangerous side-effects — but only in an enforcing mode, and only for traffic that actually passes through the proxy. It does not constrain what a model reasons about, nor any action an agent takes outside MCP.
translations.code.mcp.sim.flips: Sample decision flips vs what was recorded at ingest
translations.code.mcp.sim.hint: Dry-run the draft policy above against recent events. Nothing is saved — review the predicted decisions before committing with Save policy.
translations.code.mcp.sim.title: Simulate policy
translations.code.mcp.sim.warnBlocked: This policy would newly block {n} calls that previously proceeded.
translations.code.mcp.simSafe: No calls that previously proceeded would be newly blocked — safe to enable.
translations.code.mcp.stepEndpoint: Point the proxy at the ingest endpoint
translations.code.mcp.stepKey: Create an org API key with mcp:invoke
translations.code.mcp.stepKeyDesc: Use Settings > API Keys and choose the MCP Guardian scope. The browser never stores this key; the MCP proxy or direct ingest caller sends it as X-Flyto-API-Key.
translations.code.mcp.stepVerifyDesc: Use the dashboard probe below for a safe synthetic event, then send the curl probe or a real MCP tool call. Both paths land in Recent guardian decisions.
translations.code.mcp.testRequires: Requires mcp:configure. Records one diagnostic READ event.
translations.code.mcp.timeline.empty: No calls recorded for this session.
translations.code.mcp.timeline.hint: Reconstruct an agent session end-to-end. Paste a session id to see every tool call and the guardian decision in order.
translations.code.mcp.timeline.title: Session timeline
translations.code.mcp.title: MCP Security
translations.code.mit.claimedHint: Operator-declared but not independently verified. Click Verify to attach evidence.
translations.code.mit.coverageEmpty: No findings on the current bench match this selector. Check applies_to_tag for typos.
translations.code.mit.emptyDesc: Add the WAF rules, EDR signatures, patch baselines, or network segments that genuinely cover findings — the priority engine will discount matching rows accordingly.
translations.code.mit.evidenceEmpty: No automated checks recorded yet. The hourly evidence worker probes verification_evidence URLs — add a probe-able URL on this control to start the ledger.
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.aspirational.hint: No recent automated proof OR last probe failed — priority engine ignores this reduction.
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.aspirational.label: Aspirational
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.fading.hint: Last probe 1-7d ago. Priority engine applies 75% of the reduction.
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.fading.label: Fading
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.stale.hint: Last probe 7-30d ago. Priority engine applies 50% of the reduction.
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.stale.label: Stale
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.verified.hint: Automated probe within 24h confirms this control is live.
translations.code.mit.evidenceTier.verified.label: Auto-verified
translations.code.mit.lede: Declared mitigations reduce effective severity in the priority engine. Each row chips multiplicatively (defense-in-depth math) and caps at 85% — no claim alone closes a finding.
translations.code.mit.manager.chartReductionByType: Claimed vs Effective Reduction by Control Type
translations.code.mit.manager.queueSubtitle: Controls losing score credit because evidence is stale or aspirational
translations.code.mit.title: Compensating controls
translations.code.mit.verifyDialogDesc: Attach evidence that this control is genuinely active. Examples: a WAF rule-replay screenshot URL, an EDR alert ID, a vendor confirmation link. The priority engine still applies the reduction either way — verification flips a UI badge so your team can triage what is still claim-only.
translations.code.mit.verifyHint: Attach evidence that this control is genuinely active

translations.code.paths.drillHint: Open the CTEM Actions picker and search by domain / repo to drill into each member finding.
translations.code.paths.emptyDesc: Either the priority engine has not run yet or no findings stack into a chain. Use Recompute after the next scan to refresh.
translations.code.paths.lede: Chains across findings the priority engine judged stackable into a real attack. Each chain ranks above its loudest member.
translations.code.paths.markMitigatedHint: Hide this chain from the picker. Auto-recompute re-emits if the underlying findings still chain.
translations.code.paths.methodGraph: Bounded BFS over the asset+finding graph. Probability is the product of per-edge weights.
translations.code.paths.methodHeuristic: Pattern-matched against handcrafted chain shapes (v1). The priority number is a loudness score, not a probability.
translations.code.paths.priorityHint: Composite priority — max member + concentration bonus

translations.code.paths.type.cred_to_privesc.hint: Exposed credential plus a code-execution / SQLi finding on the same asset — broken auth instantly turns into RCE.

translations.code.paths.type.crown_jewel_concentratio: Multiple findings stacked on one high-value asset — any one is the foothold.
translations.code.paths.type.edge_to_internal.hint: External finding + code finding on the same publishing repo — classic public→source chain.

translations.code.pentest.check.ssrfDesc: Test if your server can be tricked into making requests to internal infrastructure or external systems.

translations.code.warroom.archDeadHint: Each row is a specific symbol the indexer could not find a reference to. The action column tells you how to verify before deleting — false positives are possible for dynamic imports, reflection, framework hooks, and tests.
translations.code.warroom.archImportsHint: The packages each repo leans on most. Concentrated usage = supply-chain risk if any single dep ships a bad version. Click a repo to see the full top-30.
translations.code.warroom.archMoreFiles: more files — click "Copy paths" for the full list
translations.code.warroom.archReposNone: No repository architecture data — run a scan first.
translations.code.warroom.budgetFormHint: Tokens are summed across every red-team campaign in this org. A breach pauses dispatch and opens an incident.
translations.code.warroom.budgetPoliciesEmpty: No caps yet — add one to start tracking spend.
translations.code.warroom.capabilitiesUnavailableDesc: Flyto could not verify this workspace capability snapshot. Access stays closed until the policy check succeeds.
translations.code.warroom.cicdConnectHint: Connect your CI/CD pipeline in Settings > Integrations to view build and deploy status.
translations.code.warroom.cicdConnectTitle: Connect GitHub to view CI/CD data
translations.code.warroom.cicdNone: No GitHub Actions runs found across your connected repos.
translations.code.warroom.clickToSeeCode: Click a repository to see findings
translations.code.warroom.comingSoonDesc: This feature is under development and will be available soon.
translations.code.warroom.confidenceTooltip: How sure the engine is about this verdict. Low = pattern match only — read the verification method below.
translations.code.warroom.containerCredentialHelp: Stored sealed by the engine; never returned to the browser.
translations.code.warroom.containerEmptyDesc: Container scanning detects vulnerabilities in Docker images and base layers. Connect repos with Dockerfiles and run a scan to see results here.
translations.code.warroom.containerImageRefsHelp: Separate image refs with new lines or commas.
translations.code.warroom.containerSourceKubernetesDe: Observed from a Kubernetes connector. These findings are tied to workloads that were actually listed from the cluster.
translations.code.warroom.containerSourceRegistryDesc: Scanned through a saved container connection. This proves the image source, not that a workload is running.
translations.code.warroom.containerSourceRepoManualDe: Started from a repo context for a specific image reference. This does not prove the image is deployed.
translations.code.warroom.containerSourceRepoScanDesc: Extracted from connected repository Dockerfiles or container manifests. This is code-as-container evidence, not live runtime evidence.
translations.code.warroom.containerSourceUnknownDesc: The scan result is missing a source classification. Treat it as unverified until a repo or live connector is attached.
translations.code.warroom.containerSourceVerifyDesc: Created by verify-fixed on an existing finding. It should confirm or reopen the original source, not create runtime evidence by itself.
translations.code.warroom.cspmEmpty: Cloud posture findings come from a cloud-config snapshot. Upload a snapshot via the API or wait for SDK-based live scanning (planned). Connecting a code repo alone will not populate this view.
translations.code.warroom.cspmSub: Cloud misconfiguration findings from snapshot uploads
translations.code.warroom.deadCodeNone: No dead code detected — clean codebase.
translations.code.warroom.depsConcentrationWarn: One repo accounts for the majority of uses — single-point-of-failure risk if that repo is deprecated.
translations.code.warroom.depsNone: No dependencies detected — scan some repos first.
translations.code.warroom.depsSub: Shared packages, concentration risk & supply-chain exposure
translations.code.warroom.docsApiHint: API documentation can be found in Architecture > API List.
translations.code.warroom.docsChangelogHint: Changelog generation is planned for a future release.
translations.code.warroom.docsDetailHint: Documentation coverage details are available in each repository's detail page.
translations.code.warroom.docsReadmeHint: README quality scores are available in each repository's detail page.
translations.code.warroom.docsWhitepaperHint: Whitepaper generation will be available in Studio > AI Outputs.
translations.code.warroom.dupActionHint: Extract this block into a shared function or module. If the copies have diverged slightly, unify them first, then extract.
translations.code.warroom.dupErrorHint: Check your network connection and try refreshing the page.
translations.code.warroom.dupHint: Each row is a code block that appears in two or more locations. Extract shared logic into a function, module, or utility to reduce maintenance burden and divergence risk.
translations.code.warroom.dupNone: No duplicate code detected — clean codebase.
translations.code.warroom.dupNoneDesc: Your codebase has no detected code duplication. This is a sign of good engineering practices.
translations.code.warroom.duplicatesSub: Repeated code blocks across all repos
translations.code.warroom.dynamicVerificationMethod: Dynamic probe against target_url using a GENERIC input-validation payload (SQLi-style fuzz string). A pattern-match verdict, not CVE-specific exploitation — the result shows whether the server has basic input filtering, not whether this particular CVE is reachable. Treat "SUSPECTED EXPLOITABLE" as a prompt to investigate manually.
translations.code.warroom.findingNoMatch: No symbols match the current filter.
translations.code.warroom.findingShowingFirst: Showing first 200 — narrow the filter or click Copy visible to dump the full set.
translations.code.warroom.fwDriftWarn: Same framework at different versions — coordinate upgrades or hold back the laggard.
translations.code.warroom.fwNone: No frameworks detected — scan some repos first.

translations.code.warroom.killChain.remediateNote: Infra / pentest findings are remediated outside the automated fix loop.

translations.code.warroom.liveViewUnavailableHint: Browser preview unavailable — flyto-cloud WebSocket is not configured. Campaign is still running normally; this panel only shows the screenshot stream.
translations.code.warroom.malwareEmptyDesc: Malware scanning checks your dependencies against known malicious package databases. Run a scan on your repositories to populate this view.
translations.code.warroom.malwareSub: Known malicious packages in your dependency tree
translations.code.warroom.noApiData: No API data. Run a scan to discover endpoints.
translations.code.warroom.noAuditData: No audit data. Run a scan first.
translations.code.warroom.noData: No data available. Connect and scan your repos first.
translations.code.warroom.noDepsData: No dependency data. Run a scan first.
translations.code.warroom.noModuleData: No module data. Run a scan first.
translations.code.warroom.noRepos: No repositories connected. Connect a repo to view data.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescBaseline: Read-only recon. Detects tech stack, endpoints, WAF.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescProbe: Ranks suspected vulnerabilities from the surface.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescRecheck: Re-fires confirmed findings with payload + session variance to filter false positives.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescReport: Compresses proven findings into an exec-ready report.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescVerify: Targeted payloads to confirm or reject each suspect.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineIdleHint: Click START to launch a 5-phase campaign — Baseline → Probe → Verify → Recheck → Report.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineOpenReportTip: Open print-ready HTML report — Cmd/Ctrl+P to save as PDF
translations.code.warroom.pipelineRetestTip: Re-run only the proven findings to verify fixes
translations.code.warroom.pipelineTokensTip: LLM tokens consumed across all phases
translations.code.warroom.postureLoadFailed: Could not load the posture summary
translations.code.warroom.prNone: No pull requests yet across your connected repos.
translations.code.warroom.reachEmpty: No taint analysis data — run a scan with taint enabled in flyto-indexer to populate this view.
translations.code.warroom.reachEmptyDesc: Run a scan with taint analysis enabled to populate this view.
translations.code.warroom.reachFlowsEmpty: No per-flow data — repos scanned before this feature was added show counts only. Re-scan to populate.
translations.code.warroom.reachHow: Taint analysis traces user-controlled values (sources like request.args, env vars, file reads) through your code to dangerous functions (sinks like os.system, eval, raw SQL). When a value reaches a sink without going through a sanitizer, it's an "unsanitized flow" — the most likely class of security bug. Click any flow below to see the source / sink expressions and call path.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamAIIdle: AI is standing by. Pick a target and it will analyse the attack surface.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamBackendBanner: No execution backend is online (local runner / cloud orchestrator unreachable). The 5-phase campaign cannot progress — the elapsed clock keeps time, but no phase will run until a backend is up.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamBackendOfflineTip: No execution backend reachable (local runner / cloud orchestrator). Campaigns cannot run until one is online.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamCandidatesHint: Picking one promotes it to a target and starts the campaign.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamFindingsClear: No findings on the latest scan — all checks passed.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamFindingsNoScan: Run a scan to surface findings.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamFleetEmpty: no discovery scans yet — campaign runs independently
translations.code.warroom.redTeamFleetIdle: attack fleet — pick a target to launch
translations.code.warroom.redTeamIdleMeta: pick a domain to launch a campaign
translations.code.warroom.redTeamNoDomains: No domains yet — add one on the Domains & API page first.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamPreviewEmpty: No runnable playbooks for this target
translations.code.warroom.redTeamPreviewNote: Runner validated scope + auth and planned these steps. No traffic went out.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamPreviewPickNote: Dry-run validates scope + auth but sends no traffic.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamPreviewTip: Dry-run — no traffic, just the plan
translations.code.warroom.redTeamRunnerTip: Live runner queue — in-flight executions · remaining rate-limit tokens
translations.code.warroom.redTeamStalledBanner: Campaign dispatched, but the execution backend has reported no progress. The elapsed clock is just wall-time — it does not mean work is happening. Check that an execution backend (runner / cloud orchestrator) is reachable and can call back to this engine.
translations.code.warroom.riskFormula: Risk = secrets x 3 + critical CVEs x 5 + high CVEs x 3 + findings
translations.code.warroom.rt.logEmptyBody: Live attack telemetry streams here once a campaign is running.
translations.code.warroom.rt.playbooksEmptyBody: Lock a target and the arsenal of attack playbooks loads here.
translations.code.warroom.rt.playbooksNoneBody: The campaign runs independently — playbooks appear as discovery scans land.
translations.code.warroom.runtimeEmpty: Runtime events come from an SDK installed in your running app. Create an org API key in Settings and install the SDK to start streaming events here.
translations.code.warroom.runtimeSub: Live attack detection from production SDKs
translations.code.warroom.runtimeUnavailableDesc: Flyto could not read runtime telemetry from the engine. Coverage is not confirmed until the endpoint returns fresh heartbeat or event data.
translations.code.warroom.secActionAllClear: All clear — no open security actions.
translations.code.warroom.secActionEmpty: No open security findings across this org. Run a scan to populate this list.
translations.code.warroom.secActionHint: Open items across every connected repo, ranked by severity. Click a row to jump to the affected repo. Use the filters to narrow by type, severity, or repo.
translations.code.warroom.secActionRollupTip: Indexer reports a count without per-finding detail. Open the repo to investigate.
translations.code.warroom.secNewOrgDesc: Security findings come from scans of your code. Connect a repository to start the SAST + dependency + secrets pipeline.
translations.code.warroom.secStep1: Connect a GitHub or GitLab repository
translations.code.warroom.secStep2: Wait for the first scan — usually 1-3 minutes
translations.code.warroom.sectionUnavailableDesc: The requested view is hidden by the current organization capabilities. Flyto cannot treat missing telemetry from this view as confirmed coverage.
translations.code.warroom.stepReview: Review findings in the war room
translations.code.warroom.testGuidance: Detailed unit test, E2E, and checklist views are available in Architecture > Code Quality.
translations.code.warroom.verifyAckLabel: I am authorised to send security test payloads to this URL.
translations.code.warroom.verifyDynamicHint: Send synthetic attack requests to the target URL and compare responses. Only run against infrastructure you own and are authorised to test.
translations.code.warroom.verifyDynamicWarnBody: Dynamic probes transmit SQLi-style fuzz strings to target_url. Target WAFs and SIEMs will see them — only run against staging environments or infrastructure you have explicit authorisation to test.
translations.code.warroom.verifyInvalidUrl: Must be a valid http:// or https:// URL
translations.code.warroom.verifyLibraryHint: Heads up: the scanner classified this repo as a library / SDK. If it IS a pure library, Dynamic probing proves nothing (no deployed URL). If you DO have this repo deployed — point target_url at the real deployment on the next step and proceed.
translations.code.warroom.verifyLibraryNotice: This repo is a library / SDK — there is no deployed URL to probe. Dynamic verification is disabled because HTTP probes against an unrelated domain cannot prove whether a library CVE is exploitable here. Static reachability via the import graph is the meaningful answer for library dependencies.
translations.code.warroom.verifyStaticHint: Check if the vulnerable package is imported. No HTTP traffic. Always safe.
translations.code.warroom.verifyTargetsUnavailable: Unable to load allowed dynamic targets. Dynamic verification is blocked until this list resolves.
translations.code.warroom.vulnsRedirect: View and manage all security vulnerabilities in the Issues feed.
